codeReview.summary: The TokenStore implementation in internal/oauth/token_store.go is well-structured, thoroughly tested (23 tests covering all 14 validation assertions), and correctly implements all required features: thread-safe storage with sync.RWMutex, JSON file persistence with 0600 permissions, atomic writes via temp file + rename, automatic directory creation with 0700, startup loading, graceful corrupted file handling, expired token detection with 30s clock skew margin, refresh coordination preventing concurrent refresh storms, and transient failure resilience. The code follows project conventions (gofmt, error wrapping with %w, NewXxx constructor pattern, no external test frameworks). The only non-blocking observations are minor style points on a few comment conventions.

issues.description: NeedsRefresh applies the defaultExpiryMargin to the refresh_in-based refresh check, but does NOT apply it to the 80% TTL default path. This is inconsistent — the refresh_in path uses `refreshAt.Add(-defaultExpiryMargin)` but the default path just uses `refreshAt` directly. This could be intentional (the 80% threshold already has its own safety margin), but it's worth noting for consistency.

Save() updates the in-memory token first (under write lock) then persists to disk. If the disk write fails, the in-memory cache has already been updated to the new value. This is documented behavior (validated by TestTokenStore_TransientWriteFailure), which is correct for resilience. However, callers should be aware the in-memory and on-disk states may diverge after a failed Save. Not a bug — by design.

SetRefreshError is currently a no-op (just logs). The comment correctly notes it 'can be extended for retry/backoff tracking'. This is fine for the current scope but downstream features may need to implement actual retry/backoff logic here.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Droid-Shield pre-commit hook flagged test fixture code containing AccessToken/RefreshToken field assignments as potential secrets. The worker had to restructure test helper code (using helperTokenFields with individual field assignments and comments like '// test fixture') to work around this. This is not documented in AGENTS.md and could affect other workers writing test code with sensitive-looking field names.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations notes: 'Had to restructure test fixture code to avoid Droid-Shield false positives on AccessToken/RefreshToken field assignments'. See internal/oauth/token_store_test.go helperTokenFields function with individual field assignment pattern.

summary: PASS. The oauth-token-store feature is well-implemented with clean, idiomatic Go code. All 9 required behaviors are correctly implemented: (1) thread-safe storage via sync.RWMutex for reads and separate sync.Mutex for refresh coordination, (2) 0600 file permissions verified by test, (3) atomic writes via CreateTemp + Chmod + Close + Rename with proper cleanup on each error path, (4) MkdirAll with 0700 for parent directories, (5) loadFromDisk on startup in NewTokenStore constructor, (6) corrupted/empty JSON files handled with log warning and fresh start, (7) IsExpired with 30s clock skew margin and ValidToken returning nil for expired tokens, (8) StartRefresh prevents concurrent refresh storms via refreshMu mutex + refreshing flag, (9) transient write failures leave in-memory cache updated. 23 comprehensive tests cover all validation assertions (VAL-TOKEN-013 through VAL-TOKEN-037) plus additional integration scenarios. Three non-blocking observations noted: inconsistent defaultExpiryMargin application in NeedsRefresh, intentional in-memory/disk divergence on write failure, and SetRefreshError being a no-op placeholder. No blocking issues found.
